candidates.stmt:
I used to work in financial services until 2015, when a major medical event ended my career. Over the last few years, I have been a carer for my disabled, elderly mother. Looking after my mum while managing my own health issues has shown me how hard life can be when the system doesn’t work for you. That’s why I’m standing — to be a councillor who listens and gets things done for people.
I’m an avid reader, motorsport fan, wannabe author, and cockapoo mum.
If elected, I will work hard for the people of Beauchief and Greenhill and focus on the issues that affect daily life:
• Fixing our crumbling roads
• Repairing neglected council housing
• Making our streets cleaner and safer
• Protecting our green spaces and greenbelt
• Standing up for motorists
• Supporting local businesses
• Restoring pride in Sheffield

Jonathan is a former Democratic Services Officer and School Governor with a keen interest and passion for improving both public life and Public Spaces. A lifelong Sheffielder who has campaigned on causes such as Women’s Safety in Public with the Know the Line campaign, tackling sexual harassment.

Jonathan is keen to see our local green spaces protected and improved for the benefit and usage of all residents within Batemoor, Beauchief, Greenhill, Jordanthorpe and Lowedges.

Stewart Kemp is a retired local government officer and now a volunteer community organiser working to protect, promote and improve Sheffield’s green spaces and river corridors which are essential for our physical and mental well-being as well as for nature. Since retiring he has also given time to help young children improve their reading skills, help young offenders get their lives back on track, and, through community forum work, helped to set up and run an afternoon social club for the elderly to tackle social isolation.

Stewart arrived in Sheffield in 1978 as a newly qualified probation officer, initially working in the Beighton and Mosborough areas. He is a keen cyclist, hill walker and devoted parent and grandparent.

I am a self-employed gardener who works around the city of Sheffield.  A vote for me is a vote for a Green voice in Beighton fighting hard for you and not for the wealthy few.

I am really passionate about helping create better transport links across through better cycle routes and better buses to the places you want to go and not just to the Sheffield city centre. Also, as a self-employed business owner, I want to encourage and help push for better support for young people. To help them learn new skills which will empower them to seek the jobs they want to do and to become the future of this city, keeping it moving and building a brighter future.

I have lived in Frecheville since 2003. I work as a news and features writer for a railway magazine publisher in Sheffield.

I‘m concerned about noise and pollution from traffic, cuts to bus services, lack of adequate facilities for cyclists, lack of affordable housing and Sheffield’s poor record on recycling compared to many other councils. I want better use of community assets such as Birley Spa Bath House and the recently closed St Cyprian's church.

Independent Candidate

My name is David Cronshaw and I’m happy to announce that I will be standing in Mays Local Elections on 7th May  as a Independent Candidate — Aligned with Restore Britain in The Birley Ward

Vote for common sense. Vote for real change.

No Building on Greenbelt: Protecting our green spaces for future generations.

More Youth Clubs: Giving our children and teenagers safe spaces and opportunities to thrive.

Cancel Clean Air Zone: Scrap unfair schemes that hit local people and businesses in the pocket.

More Police on the Streets: Safer neighbourhoods, less crime, more visible policing.

Say YES to Social Housing: Prioritising residents and fighting for affordable homes in our community.

Support Local Businesses: Backing our high streets and independent traders — the backbone of S12.

Cancel Net Zero Target of 2030: Sensible, achievable environmental policies — not rushed targets that do not work for us.

I am proud to live and work in S12 and have done so for over 25 years. I understand what matters to our community because I am part of it

In 2016, I founded the S12 Community Page, which now brings together 49,000 members which helps neighbours support each other and making local voices heard.

I support the Royal British Legion every year, fundraising for our Tommies and honouring our veterans. This year, you will see six Tommies around S12 as a mark of respect and remembrance.

Let’s Restore Britain, Starting in Birley Ward

Vote Independent for your local champion. Vote David Cronshaw in May 2026.
Your vote, your community, your future.
———————————————————
Contact Details:
David Cronshaw
Tel 07435 443585
Email davidcronshaw2024@outlook.com

Promoted by D.Cronshaw
8 Birley Moor Crescent, Sheffield, S12 3AS

X @birleysheffield

Threads @birleywardsheffield
Snapchat @birleysheffield
TikTok @birleywardsheffield

Broomhill and Sharrow Vale is my home. It is where I was raised, went to primary school and had my first job. I still live here today.

Before becoming a councillor, I did community and youth work for many years. I care deeply about these matters, especially supporting young people and families with children who have disabilities or special educational needs.

What keeps me grounded is the local community. I still volunteer in the ward and spend as much time as I can speaking to residents and helping with issues. Being a councillor is not just about meetings at the Town Hall. It is about listening, standing up for people and doing the small practical things that make our neighbourhood a better place to live.

Broomhill and Sharrow Vale shaped who I am, and it is a privilege to represent the place I call home.

My name is Mustafa Ahmed, and I’ve lived with my family on the Wensley Estate for over 25 years. I am proud to be standing in the upcoming council elections to offer Burngreave a clear alternative to Labour and to stand up for our community. I know our area well – and I care deeply about it.

**My work for our community**

For many years, I’ve been actively involved in improving life for local residents.

- Within our Tenants and Residents Association, I’ve worked to make residents’ voices heard and get local priorities met.
- I’ve helped many neighbours access crucial support systems, such as housing advice, health services and family support.
- For the Wensley Park Restoration project, I helped raise nearly £40,000 to improve our community space.
- Volunteering with Sheffield Grand Mosque, we’ve delivered community programmes, youth activities, and educational trips that strengthen community spirit.
- Through the Fee Sabilillah Funeral Service, I support bereaved families during their most difficult times.
- I’ve connected many neighbours to crucial support networks, and I often help run volunteer events like litter-picking, children’s activities, and awareness campaigns because small actions make a big difference.
- I’ve run or assisted at many projects within our community, from litter picking to awareness campaigns to children’s activities
- While out in our area, I have been reporting fly-tipping and pressuring the council to take action

**I can win and bring hope back to Sheffield**

Our mosques, churches and community spaces are places where people from all backgrounds come together, and this diversity is the very heart of Burngreave. But in the last few years, Labour has stoked racism with its anti-immigrant policies. They have chosen to build more weapons instead of funding the NHS. They have neglected local communities like ours. And even now, they still permit weapons sales to Israel and collaborate with the Israeli military.

I believe politics should

Abdullah Okud — From Burngreave. For Burngreave.

Abdullah Okud is a community organiser, youth worker, trade unionist, philanthropist, and lifelong Burngreave resident. He grew up here, works here, and fights here.

With over 10 years of experience from the civil service to the voluntary sector—Abdullah has dedicated his life to serving people, helping organisations secure funding, empowering young people, and building stronger communities.

From organising Black Lives Matter protests in Sheffield to standing for Palestine and Sudan, Abdullah has never backed down from the struggle for justice. He has supported local youth clubs, grassroots football, litter campaigns, and community initiatives because real change starts on our streets.

Burngreave has been neglected for too long. Abdullah is standing as an Independent, backed by YourParty, the Muslim Vote, Vote Palestine Campaign to fight for investment, opportunity, and dignity for our people.

Real leadership. Real justice. Real change.

Vote Abdullah Okud — a voice for the community, from the community.

I’m Maia! I’m writing to ask for your support in the May council elections. I live in City Ward, in Highfield. I worked for St Mary’s Community Centre for 8½ years, have been on the local Tenants’ and Residents’ committee for 14 years and brought my children up here. In that time, I’ve run lots of projects aimed at making people’s lives better in the local community.

**Working hard all year round**

- I wrote the successful funding bid for new play equipment and landscaping of Duchess Road pocket park.
- I’ve worked on poverty, mental health awareness and youth unemployment and campaigned against disability benefit restrictions. I’ve developed and managed projects in communities across Sheffield to build community, engage volunteers and reduce food poverty.
- I am working hard to join your elected Green councillors, Douglas Johnson and Ruth Mersereau, and to follow in the footsteps of Councillor Martin Phipps. We work year-round in community groups, speak to local businesses, knock on people’s doors for a chat, and try to make everyone feel included and heard.

**Bringing back hope into politics**

Across the country, the Green Party is growing, bringing hope back into politics. Hope means moving towards a politics rooted in integrity, compassion, and action, like we’ve done in Sheffield for over 20 years now.

**This local election is about local issues and it’s between Greens and Labour here.**

Greens offer a real alternative to Labour. We’re active all year round and achieve real benefits for our residents. City Ward deserves a future shaped by the people who live here and love this area. Together, we can build it.
Local businessman in the City ward who wants to help change the area for the better. Currently feeds many of the local homeless folk and wants to bring sanity to the Council.

I first came to Crookes & Crosspool a decade ago while studying at university, and fell in love with the area. I'm honoured to be your Labour & Co-operative councillor and to have the opportunity to serve our community.

I first stood for election because I wanted more action on housing, public transport, economic justice, and the climate justice. In that time I have introduced policies that have made our local economy fairer, and I have worked to ensure local services protect the most vulnerable.

After over a decade of austerity, I know there is much more to be done, and I hope you will continue to put your faith in me to stand up for social, economic, and climate justice at a local and national level.

Dylan Lewis-Creser is your candidate in Crookes and Crosspool for the local election on May 7th.

Dylan has lived in Crookes for 3 years, and is passionate about improving our neighbourhood's public transport, urban design, and reducing inequality in our area.

They said: "Like so many of us, I chose Sheffield as my home because of our community spirit, green spaces, and commitment to social justice.

We deserve a city council that works for all of us - voting for me means you get a councillor that understands and that will actively listen to our communities, and work hard to make change."

Ricia grew up by Crosspool’s crags with horses in fields lining lanes now absorbing Malin Bridge bottleneck-avoiding traffic, making it too dangerous to cycle, ride or walk along. 
 
 ”Motorists burn extra fuel taking circuitous routes I could safely cross to ballet school and church halls where, at 10, I saw Joe Cocker. I still support local music:  RiciaBHC Youtube shorts.” 
 
Post Notre Dame High School, Ricia graduated from Leeds University and taught at Shirecliffe College before going to Petroleum Development Oman where she became Ricia: Arabic for feather.” 
 
Then in Boston Mass. she gained an MA while working as a journalist, leading to Economist Newspaper Ltd assignments overseas making her very conscious of what draws and deters investment.  
 
“To pay for all its residents demand and politicians promise to win votes, Sheffield must be more enterprise-friendly to increase productivity per capita and ensure resources aren’t wasted. Cities need those who understand this at the helm. Lenders say no as risks of defaulting rise.” 
 
A local live music supporting landlady agrees: “Sheffield must be run like a good pub, keeping customers happy but turning a profit, otherwise there’s no money for staff.”

My name is Joydu Al-Mahfuz. I am  54 years old and a self-employed business owner. I have lived in Darnall for 28 years.

I want to be a councillor to change Darnall.  I want to hear your ideas on how we can make Darnall safer, greener & cleaner. We deserve to live better and more peacefully, no matter what our race,nationality,language or gender is. I believe everybody should have a voice, and your opinions are valuable – I am here to listen. Most of my time, I am working closely with local people, and I have a good understanding of how this area needs to be improved. So a vote for me is a vote for you.

I’m a local business owner, and I have lived within 2 miles of Dore and Totley my whole life and a proud father and grandfather who cares deeply about the future of our community. Having lived and worked here in Sheffield for decades, I’ve seen first-hand how decisions made at the Town Hall affect everyday life — and right now, too many of those decisions are failing local people.

Sheffield is facing a serious challenge. The council is over £1.2 billion in debt, with hundreds of thousands of pounds being spent every week just on interest. That’s money not being used to fix our roads, improve services, or support residents — it’s simply paying for past mismanagement. Meanwhile, our streets are crumbling, services are declining, and residents are being asked to pay the price.

This isn’t sustainable — and it isn’t acceptable.

Across the city, people can see things aren’t working. Expensive schemes like so-called “Dutch-style” roundabouts are confusing and ineffective. Traffic management systems have made congestion worse, not better, leaving drivers stuck in queues day after day. At the same time, parking charges in the city centre have become so high that they are driving people away — hurting local businesses and contributing to boarded-up shops and declining footfall.

We’ve also seen poorly designed cycle lanes that are rarely used yet have reduced road capacity and added to congestion. Recycling centres are often miles away, difficult to access, and operate limited hours — making it harder, not easier, for residents to do the right thing.

The list goes on — and it all points to the same problem: decisions being made without proper thought for the people who live and work here.

That’s why I’m standing in the council election: to bring accountability, common sense, and real delivery back into local government.

A key priority for me is fixing our roads properly and promptly. Residents are fed up with potholes, broken surfaces, and repeated short-term fixes that

I joined the Green Party 12 years ago to try to address unfairness, inequality and the climate emergency. I am a retired Local Authority senior manager and have had several roles in the Party, including Ward Organiser and as a member of the Executive.

I’m standing in this election as I am seeking to join other Green councillors in implementing sustainable policies for Sheffield. I support plans for Active Travel and a reduction in air pollution. I have a disabled husband, so I am very aware that any developments need to include consultation with and provision for disabled people. Other priorities are to increase recycling, to improve access to effective building insulation, renewable energy and other measures to reduce the use of fossil fuels.

If elected as your local Councillor, I would listen to and work on behalf of all residents across all areas of the Dore and Totley ward.

Hi, I’m Nicola Smith, your candidate for Labour in Dore & Totley Ward.
If you choose to vote for me, I promise to do my best and will work hard. I am honest and have a wide variety of experience which I will use to ensure our ward gets the very best representation.
I founded a successful tech business which has been running 8 years, real proof I understand some of the difficulties affecting local businesses. I am also someone that moves with the times and know when modern technology and how it can be utilised for good.
As a school governor at Totley Primary School, I’ve learnt a huge amount about our education system and our local schools. I have pushed forward the healthy eating and have been the link governor for the community, early years, and PSHE.
I've been campaigning and making real change all my life. My first successful campaign age 12 saw my school uniform change. I founded a charity week that has ran 6 times and spread to 3 continents. It was mentioned in the House of Lord. Locally I campaigned to slow down the traffic on my road, which is now a 20-mph limit and for more blue bin space, which is happening in the coming months. I have bags of energy and often find if you push from many angles, if the change is needed the door it happens. 
As a proud Yorkshire woman, passionate about building strong, connected communities. I’ve lived in Totley with my husband and daughters.
From the moment we arrived, we knew we’d made the right choice to live here. Our ward has an incredible sense of community, beautiful landscapes on our doorstep, and fantastic local schools where our daughters now learn and grow. Being part of this community has inspired me every day to help make our neighbourhood stronger, more inclusive, and supportive for everyone who calls it home.
I’m actively involved in local life: As well as serving as a school governor, I manage a Porter Girls football team and am a leader at Beaver Scouts. You’ll see me manning stalls at local events, joining l

I’m committed to campaigning for social justice, for tackling climate change, and progressive solutions to the challenges of our turbulent times. I’m a student nurse, an NHS Responder, a school governor, and dad to four young children.

I have spent 4 years as Co-chair of a city-wide climate change and environmental campaign charity.

I am standing for election to serve my local community as a candidate of the Labour and the Co-operative parties.

I am currently heading up a project to provide free energy advice and support to some of the most financially vulnerable people in our communities, people who have been failed by successive governments and banks.

I’m standing as the Green Party candidate for East Ecclesfield because our community deserves better, better services, better representation, and a stronger voice in the decisions that shape our lives.

With a background in healthcare, I’ve seen firsthand the impact that economic inequality, underfunded services, and poor planning have on people and communities. Too often, decisions are made without listening to those most affected. I’m standing to change that.

As a Green Party councillor, I will push for a fairer East Ecclesfield, where public services work for those who most need them, and where future generations have real economic opportunities to thrive. That means listening properly, standing up for what matters locally, and challenging decisions that don’t serve our community, whether that’s cuts to services, poor public transport, or a lack of investment in our area.

I’m Tessa, a local mum, a worker in the not-for-profit sector and your Green candidate in the May local council elections. I grew up in Ecclesall and now want to give back to this vibrant community.

**I can win and help bring hope back to Sheffield**

Across the country, more people are responding to the Green Party’s message of bringing hope back into politics. Here in Ecclesall, residents tell me they want a councillor who listens, who understands family life, and who will stand up for the area.

As a granddaughter, I supported my grandparents through Vascular Dementia. As a daughter, I lost my Mum when I was 24 and dealt with grief. As a mum, I’ve campaigned for cleaner air, more green spaces, play spaces for children and safer streets. I feel a deep compassion for people of all ages through my lived experience. I want Ecclesall to be a place where every family feels safe, supported, and glad to call home.

**Working for you all year round**

If elected, I would be proud to work alongside Green councillor Peter Gilbert, who won the last local elections in Ecclesall Ward. We will speak up for residents in council meetings, challenge decisions that don’t reflect our community’s needs, and work hard to deliver positive, practical change. Peter and I are involved with local community groups, speak with local businesses, and knock on doors to listen and support.

**Your vote counts on 7th May.**

Ecclesall deserves a future shaped by the people who live here and love this area. On 7th May, please use your vote to elect me as your representative on Sheffield City Council — so together, we can build a cleaner, safer, more hopeful future.

I have lived in close proximity to Ecclesall for all my life, I appreciate what a privilege it is to live in such a green and pleasant area of our city.

I am, however, concerned to see the negative changes in our city, and, if elected, would fight against further changes to the detriment of Sheffield’s heritage.

If elected I would campaign and be proactive in voting to protect our city’s green belt and community areas, and promote development on brown field sites, and renovating the approximately 7000 empty properties around the city, and making full use of buildings like Moorfoot and Park Hill flats, which are standing half empty.  By voting en masse for the Sheffield Plan, the Green Party Councillors have ignored the voices of thousands of residents across the city this plan will directly affect.

Probably the most common theme canvassers have encountered on the doorstep is potholes. The roads in Sheffield are terrible, and any repairs are shoddy and temporary at best, and not fit for purpose.  Contractors need to shape up or be replaced. Sheffield has businesses collapsing due to the clean air zone charges, and lack of parking. Tradesmen are unable work in the city centre due to the ULEZ charges.  Because of this the residential roads skirting the charging zone have much higher traffic and poorer air quality as a consequence.  How can this be seen as beneficial for our residents?  We need to have sensible, non-punitive policies, and stop the war on motorists. Council spending on white elephant projects such as the Dutch roundabouts, which hardly sees any cyclists, are seen as vanity projects and a waste of money that could be spent on important services.

Anti-social behaviour in the city is getting worse. Electric bikes and scooters being driven dangerously in busy areas and through shopping arcades. Parks and open
spaces are no longer safe spaces for small children to play.  Areas in the city are covered in Graffiti. There is a lack of local policing. We need

I’m Fran, an experienced community activist with a track record of bringing investment into local services.
I’m standing to be your councillor to ensure residents in Walkley, Upperthorpe and Netherthorpe have a real say in the decisions that affect their lives and are 
empowered to improve our community.

I’m proud to have worked for the NHS for over 13 years, As a councillor I want to work with local people to foster community-led regeneration, that reflects the needs, wants and aspirations of Firth Park residents. Our area deserves investment to improve roads, parks, safety and a better quality of life.

Mike Harrison has worked mostly in IT but also spent time as a teaching assistant. Since retirement, he has offered his IT experience to a solar panel charity, a community hub, some housing co-ops and a small voluntary organisation.

In his spare time, Mike has performed with an ensemble of traditional storytellers to both adults and children. He enjoys walking, music and movies.

He is passionate about affordable housing, human rights, fair taxation and spending, and ensuring our great-grandchildren can live in a safe climate and flourishing natural world.

I aim to join a growing group of Green councillors making local action to address the climate emergency their priority. Greens will continue to campaign for better public transport and safer cycling and walking options so people can choose to leave their cars at home, reducing congestion for all road users while improving dangerous air pollution and road safety.

There is a clear need to turn around the lack of reliable public transport options to allow people to access both the Northern General and Hallamshire hospitals. Greens are among many campaigners who have now got this high up the agenda of the South Yorkshire Mayor, who is responsible for bus services and needs to make this an urgent priority

John Ronan has lived in Fulwood for 35 years, running his own locally-based landscaping and gardening business for 25 years. His daughter attended local schools.

John volunteers with Derbyshire Wildlife Trust and is a keen bird enthusiast and experienced runner. John cares deeply about the local area including local parks, nature and green spaces and is concerned about the state of the country and the world at the moment.

As a Father to 3 young children I am wanting a safe and greener place for them to grow up in with the hope of jobs and a future here.
I’m a British Army veteran having served with the Royal Corps of Signals and since leaving the forces I have set up and run a small business that has been active since 2013.  Alongside the business I also work for the Ambulance service doing what I love to do most which is help people wherever I can.  
I believe with a passion that things can be better and for that reason I am standing for local council to start making a difference.

I’m Marieanne, I live locally with my children and cats, and I share your ambitions and your frustrations for Heeley, Meersbrook and Gleadless Valley. This May, I am standing for re-election and I hope to continue working with my Green colleagues Alexi Dimond and Paul Turpin.

It is a challenging time with successive governments continuing austerity and the rising cost of living – that’s why Greens work in our communities, for our communities, year-round – not just when there is a vote to be won.

Greens offer a different kind of politics – one rooted in hope, integrity, compassion and action. For me, this is a commitment to the place I call home, standing up for people and all our different communities here.

**Action Where It Matters**

As your Green Councillor over the last four years, I have focused on positive action across our area, such as:

- Help with Cost of Living: Championing “Income Maximisation”; bringing free face-to-face advice to our community centres in Heeley Green and Newfield Green to help people get lower bills. 
- Supporting residents: Casework to help people through homelessness, timely housing repairs and maintenance in Gleadless Valley and more action on street repairs like potholes across the ward.
- Improving our environment: Acting on issues in parks, woodlands, waterways and highways. Pushing for more spaces for young people, school streets and safe routes to school.
- Community action: Supporting play streets and yard sales. Collaborating with amazing grass roots organisations on community skip days, river clean ups, girls football in Herdings Park, nature recovery in Meersbrook Park and backing community ambitions for Meersbrook Hall.

I am pushing for investment in Gleadless Valley and for solar energy on homes, schools and new leisure centres. I have challenged poor planning applications, spoken out for disabled people and carers, called for ethical investment and championed beaver reintroduction and nature recovery initiatives.

It

Andy Rayner - Gleadless Valley

For years I have been watching in anger and frustration at politicians making our lives, our country and our city worse.  And I’ve had enough!

So, instead of complaining to my family & friends, or shouting at the TV!, I’ve decided that I need to do something positive about it, which is to join Reform in striving for a better Sheffield & Gleadless Valley.

Who am I?

I live locally with my wife and kids, who are still at school.
I’m now retired, but I worked for many years in both the private and public sector. I have years of experience in managing teams, budgets and projects, and I believe the skills and knowledge I have acquired would help a great deal in the sometimes complex world of local government.
I enjoy sport, both watching and playing, (as much as my ageing body allows)!
I moved to Sheffield in 1991 - I love it here, the people are great - it’s my adopted home. And the surrounding countryside is a gift which me and my family enjoy

What do I believe in?

I believe in having a smaller government that interferes less in our lives.
I believe in personal and parental responsibility.
I believe in harsher consequences for lawbreakers and anti-social behaviour.
I believe in greater accountability and consequences for public officials who show themselves to be incompetent or dishonest.
I try to be fair in everything I say and do.
I do NOT believe in a climate “emergency”, or the need for a fake ‘net zero’ which simply pushes emissions to other parts of the world, at great expense to UK citizens.
Unlike the Greens, Lib Dems and some of the Labour party, I know what a woman is! Women’s rights, private spaces and women’s sports must be protected.
I believe in free speech and am alarmed at how much it is under attack from this Labour government. 
I believe in our country and our culture, and we need to be careful not to lose it.

What do I want for Gleadless Valley?

I want a Sheffield Council that delivers all of its basic services e

I’m Tom Atkin-Withers, a local resident just like you, and your Green Party candidate for Graves Park in the upcoming 7th May local elections.

I’m a charity trustee working with young people, and through that work I see first-hand the challenges facing families in our area, across our city, and nationally. I’m also an active member of the Graves Park community, and what I hear most often on the doorstep is frustration about poor and inaccessible public transport across the ward, and despair that people aren’t being listened to. Frankly, it makes me as angry as it makes you.

**I actively stand up for Graves Park**

The current Labour and Liberal Democrat councillors have failed to stand up for Graves Park residents. I want to change that. Over the last year alone, driven by concerns raised by local people, I have:
- Worked with Green councillors to push for better transport links between Graves Park and Chesterfield Hospital
- Secured repairs to damaged bus stops across our area
- Helped secure the prompt resurfacing of the new crossing by Meadowhead School after a resident was injured
- Launched a petition to get Osmaston Road adopted and resurfaced
- Worked to keep our green spaces litter free

**Unlike other parties Greens drive change**

While Labour and the Lib Dems keep things the same, the Greens are working hard to improve the city we all love. From Climate Bonds that allow residents to fund solar panels on our children’s schools, to protecting our environment from toxic pesticides and fighting for better-funded public transport, we’re focused on practical action that improves everyday life.

**I can win here and bring hope back to Sheffield.**

Neighbouring wards like Gleadless Valley, Nether Edge & Sharrow, and Ecclesall already have Green councillors, and across the country the Green Party is growing as more people respond to a hopeful, honest alternative to politics as usual.

As your councillor, my priority would be simple: delivering local results. St

Graves Park Ward – Community First Manifesto

Putting Residents Back at the Heart of Decisions

Graves Park Ward is one of the most valued and distinctive parts of Sheffield. From its green spaces to its strong community spirit, it deserves leadership that listens, protects, and delivers.
This campaign is built on a simple promise: local people come first.

1. Protecting Our Quality of Life
Residents deserve to feel proud of where they live. That means tackling the everyday issues that affect daily life:
* Safer streets – Working with police and local groups to reduce anti-social behaviour and improve community safety
* Cleaner neighbourhoods – Prioritising street cleaning, tackling fly-tipping, and improving waste services
* Smarter traffic solutions – Addressing congestion, speeding, and unsafe junctions with practical, locally informed measures
Our goal: A cleaner, safer, and more liveable Graves Park Ward for everyone.

2. Defending the Character of Our Area
Graves Park Ward is unique – and that must be protected.
* Opposing overdevelopment that erodes green space or local identity
* Ensuring planning decisions respect the history and character of our communities
* Protecting valued public spaces for future generations
Our commitment: No change without respect for what makes our area special.

3. Real Community Voice in Council Decisions
Too many residents feel decisions are made about them, not with them.
* Introducing genuine public consultation on major local decisions
* Giving residents a stronger voice in planning and development
* Ensuring local concerns are heard early—not ignored until it’s too late
Our promise: You will be listened to—and your views will matter.

4. Restoring Trust and Transparency
Trust in the council has been damaged by secrecy and unclear decision-making.
* Ending “behind closed doors” decisions wherever possible
* Publishing clear, accessible information on council plans and land use
* Holding decision-makers accountable to resident

I'm Julia, I work in social housing, enabling elderly and vulnerable residents to receive the support they need.
I'm standing for Council to give something back to the city and community that raised and supported me and my family, and to ensure future generations have the same opportunities that Sheffield gave me. I want to be part of a Council that is proud to provide good quality housing and improves the quality of life and the prospects for all its residents.
I've been out listening on the doorstep, and at events, to hear what is important to you and how I can support you all. I've taken part in community events in Graves Park and Woodseats and I have been working alongside your Labour Councillor Rob Bannister. I'm committed to working hard for everyone in our area.
I am passionate about improving opportunities and living standards for all. As a Labour councillor I would be able to work with stakeholders to challenge inequalities and put policies into place to remove discrimination and offer support.

I’m Richard, your Green candidate in the May local council elections. I lived off Leppings Lane for 19 years and now live in Wadsley Bridge. I walk my son to Hillsborough Primary School and I’m passionate about improving this area, which I know very well. Hillsborough Greens are:

**Working for you year round**

I work alongside Green councillors Christine Gilligan Kubo, Toby Mallinson and Henry Nottage (standing down this year) to improve local people’s lives. Your councillors worked hard to get the 52A bus back and are pushing the Labour mayor for improved, reliable, bus services and proper maintenance of tram tracks.

**Supporting local communities**

Hillsborough Greens’ actively engage with the local community, supporting organisations doing good work in our communities and giving local people a voice. The climate emergency drives many of our actions.

I worked for local community service provider Zest for over a decade until recently. I want to use my experience gained from working in collaboration with the council and charities to help our communities, especially people struggling to pay their bills. Households have been let down by the Labour government.

**I will strive to get the best for Hillsborough by pushing forward positive change.**

I have lived in the Norfolk Park area of Sheffield since 2011. I have a wealth of experience in my working life: I've been an advisor at Citizens Advice; a university librarian; an NHS administrator and receptionist and an IT support team manager. I currently volunteer at S2 Food Bank as a debt mentor, helping Food Bank clients to deal with their financial problems. I also run a weekly collection for food bank donations.

I joined the Green Party after taking part in the campaign to save our local street trees. As a relative newcomer to Sheffield, I know that I am privileged to live in such a green city, and was really moved by the way that local Greens worked within the community to protect our environment during the street trees campaign.

I am a keen cyclist, walker and user of public transport, and have been car-free since 2017. As I walk and cycle around our ward, I'm struck by how much the place we live affects our mental health - our ward deserves to be both clean and green - there's a lot of work to do there. I regularly litter-pick around our ward, and organise a monthly litter-pick on the Manor. But I know that more needs to be done. I am particularly concerned by the poor air quality in Sheffield and the effect it has on our health and the health of children in the city.

I think that Sheffield City Council should reflect the best of Sheffield: our care for others; our strong sense of community and our plain-speaking approach to life. Those are the values that make this such a great place to live.

If elected as Manor Castle's first Green councillor, I would welcome the chance to dedicate myself full-time to the residents of Manor Castle ward, and to help bring the values of the Green Party to Sheffield City Council. Those are the values that led me to join the Greens: a commitment to our environment and a belief in an equal chance for everybody, whatever their background or way of life.

I live in Manor Castle and know our communities and what they need. I have spent my whole professional career representing and helping people that need it most and I want to use that to help people in my area.

Our communties are strong and diverse and show that working together we can get things done. I will stand up to the kind of hatred and division that is trying to tear them down rather than build them up.

I want to join our current councillors Elle Dodd and Laura Moynahan who are already working so hard across the ward on projects to help improve the area. I am determined and hard working and will ensure everyone has a voice at Town Hall.

I am a GP and an outdoor enthusiast. I live in Sheffield with my partner and our elderly adopted dog. I have been campaigning on issues relating to health, human rights and the environment for more than 20 years.

I’m a home carer who has been a tenant of the Lansdowne in Sharrow for around twenty years. For the last year, I have been working closely wih Cllr Maroof Raouf. We work hard for our local community, and I am so happy we have managed to make an impact in the time I have supported Maroof.

Alongside this, I have worked independently on projects with the Local Area Committee - improving the play area in Lansdowne -, and as a founding member of Action For Lansdowne, a tenants’ union in the pursuit of better-maintained homes.

I also lead two local litterpicking teams, work I really enjoy and value. One usually sticks to Lansdowne itself, and the other typically covers the Highfield/Lowfield area. Between the two teams, we go out 5 times a month to help bring some local pride to our area and to make it safe for people and wildlife.

I am also a seasoned campaigner. I organised two protests and brought the community together to raise hundreds of objections to a McDonald’s planning application in Lowfield, ultimately resulting in their application being rejected.

Growing up in Sheffield, I’ve been lucky enough to live in our green city my whole life and feel a strong responsibility to preserve our woodlands, rivers and parks for future generations. This includes a wider commitment to our national resources. As such, it is only natural that I very much support the renationalisation of our water supply back into public hands.

I joined Sheffield Green Party over a decade ago to protect public services and spaces, ensure everyone has access to decent housing, and to support the creation of green jobs that help to build a strong local economy that works for us all. I am incredibly proud to be standing for election, and I cannot wait to begin the work in Council.

I'm a Mental Health Recovery Worker, founder of a local charity and passionate advocate for diversity and inclusion within the Labour party and beyond. My voluntary work with vulnerable families throughout the pandemic and the challenges I have seen firsthand across our community have motivated me to stand for councillor to make a real difference in people's lives during this difficult time.

Joanne is a well experienced Chair of various committees from “Stop Hate Scrutiny Panel” with South Yorkshire Police to the “Choices not Barriers” panel over four years.

Joanne is a Criminologist and dedicated researcher into online stalking and stalking.

Joanne is setting up a new podcast

“Trailblazing Women in Law”

The podcast aims to interview investigators, lawyers, academics and politicians on their personal and professional experiences within in the criminal justice system.

Joanne’s top goals are to achieve:

1. Safer, cleaner, and determined to address fly tipping. 
2.  To encourage more visible policing and listening to the community issues.
3.  Zero tolerance to anti social behaviour, hate crime and online harassment. 
4. To encourage more interaction with people who have not been involved in the local theatres, artists and musicians. 
5. Encouraging and engaging in local businesses and new businesses.
6. Standing up for council accountability in relation to preserving trees and the green spaces. 
7. Building a community where everyone can thrive.

I'm a 23-year-old engineer and I want to fix our great Steel City. For decades Labour have taken us for granted and ran our city into the ground, but that ends now. I will fight to make our streets safe again, prioritise homes for local people, fix the potholes and support local businesses. Only Reform can beat Labour in this ward, and if elected I will fight for you and your family on the Council. Sheffield needs Reform! 🇬🇧

I’m Nabeela, and I’m honoured to be your Labour & Co-operative councillor. I first stood for election because I grew up and live here. I wanted more action on housing, transport, public services, the cost of living and youth support.

I’m proud of the changes we have seen in that time: bringing buses under public control, free breakfast clubs in our primary schools, investing £51 million to rebuild Springs Leisure Centre, and ambitious policies to make our local economy fairer while delivering services that protect the most vulnerable.

On Thursday 7th May, I am standing for re-election to continue serving as your local councillor.

Representing you as your local councillor for the last four years has been the privilege of my life and I have worked to ensure that your views are represented in the Town Hall.  I’m so proud of the work we have done together, but I know there is much more to be done.

Some in this election will be seeking to divide our community. I want to keep standing up for our area, and keep fighting for the issues that matter to you. I’m here for every resident, and will continue to stand up and demand better for you and your families.

I was born and raised in Sheffield, mainly in the S2 area. I’m a single mother of two now living on Daresbury Road, close to East Bank Road.

I know the area like the back of my hand. I cycle for everything except the odd local dog walk. I usually buy my food locally, except what I manage to grow and raise in my garden. The best bit is eggs from my hens!

I was homeless for long periods as a teenager. I’ve had long periods in my life where I had to choose between heating and eating. I now live in a low-income household, and I’m dependent on government financial top-up and benefits.

I want to make The Park and Arbourthorne council ward a family-friendly neighbourhood as this goes hand in hand with accessibility. I understand the importance of improvements and change. With happiness comes health, and with health comes happiness. As a bus user, I know how vital good services within walking distance are to people living in this area.

I’m standing to be your local councillor for the Green Party because they understand the needs of everyday people like me.  We will keep fighting for households in the area, and across Sheffield, to receive the help and support they need in an ongoing cost-of-living crisis.

Just a little about me, I'm a working-class lorry driver who clocks up 50 to 60 hours a week behind the wheel to provide for my other half and our soon-to-be newborn son. 
My priorities have always been simple: my family, my faith, and my country.

I’m deeply worried about the way things are going. Our community and our country feel like they’re slipping. Antisocial behaviour is everywhere - kids hanging around causing trouble, shoplifting treated like it’s no big deal, and vehicle theft along with illegal motorcycles tearing around our streets night after night. I don’t know about you, but I’m sick of it. At the same time, our council tax goes up every single year, yet we see no extra police on the beat, no cleaner streets, and no real help for the problems that matter most to ordinary families.

That’s why I’ve decided to stand as an independent candidate for the local councillor position in Park & Arbourthorne. I don’t have fancy degrees or political connections - just a strong sense that enough is enough. I want to see if someone who actually lives and works here can make a difference and improve the quality of life for all of us. We deserve to feel safe in our homes and in our community.

If I’m elected, I will work flat out to turn things around. No empty promises - just honest, determined action to tackle the crime, cut the waste, and put local people first again.

My name is Beth Cheshire, and I’m the Labour and Co-operative Party candidate for Richmond ward in the upcoming election on 7 May.

**So why vote for me?**

I’m a local candidate - I live in S13 with my husband and our toddler - and I think that’s important. I want this area to be the best it can be because that’s what we all deserve, but also because it’s where my family lives and where my child will grow up.

What an area being the best it can be looks like will look different to everyone of course, but for me it means:

🔴Clean, safe streets, where we can let our kids play without worrying about speeding traffic or stepping in dog muck.
🔴Roads that don’t wreck your suspension with endless potholes.
🔴Public transport that works and is affordable.
🔴Using the resources we have properly - no more empty buildings left to rot.
🔴High quality play parks and youth clubs for every area.
🔴Community spaces that work for the community, and are led by them.
🔴Accessible green spaces, protected biodiversity and clean air - which of course includes opposing the inappropriate greenbelt development in S13.

I’ve never been a councillor before - but new voices means new ideas. I won’t tell you I can magically fix every problem overnight, but what I can say is that after knocking on thousands of doors across the ward over the last year, I have a good idea of what people here care about, and that would always be my first priority.

I’m sure I’ll speak to many more of you before 7th May, but If you have any questions for me, the quickest way to contact me is by email.

**You can reach me at beth4richmond@gmail.com. Thank you!**

I was born in Sheffield and am a Doctor at the Northern General Hospital, working on sustainable healthcare projects alongside clinical work.

I stand for election as a climate-conscious citizen, committed to a healthy and sustainable future where everyone can thrive. I believe climate justice is inseparable from social and economic justice. If you elect me, I will represent your concerns in council, and campaign for improving the local environment, increasing local public transport, and investing in local public services.

I’ve lived in Sheffield my whole life, and I’m standing to be your Labour councillor for Shiregreen and Brightside because our communities deserve a strong voice and practical action.

I’m a teacher, and I’ve worked in schools across our city, so I’m used to listening, bringing people together, and getting things done with limited resources.

My priorities are the basics: tackling litter and fly-tipping, standing up for tenants and residents, dealing with crime and anti-social behaviour, and making our neighbourhoods feel cared for again.

These elections aren't about Westminster. They're about our city, our communities, and a local voice that will put you first. Use your vote for a local champion who will stand up for you.

My name is Joel Gilbert, and I am looking to be your new Green councillor for Shiregreen and Brightside this May.

Being local to the area, I care deeply about making this wonderful ward the best it can be. This means working to bring communities together to support local area improvements, whether that be playground equipment or the natural environment, as well as being a strong voice for the ward on the city council, advocating for cleaner streets, safer roads, improved services and better transport links.

My professional life has seen me be a P.E. teacher and a primary school classroom teacher (including in Shiregreen and Brightside), and I have worked with charities and social enterprises in Sheffield to plant more trees on Sheffield’s streets and in our parks. I am currently conducting research on how to make walking and cycling to school easier, safer and more pleasant for children in Sheffield.

More generally, I truly believe we are at a crossroads in politics in this country at the moment. The two traditional main parties are in freefall, and when considering who should replace them, only one party offers hope we can all believe in.

Vote Green for Shiregreen and Brightside on May 7th 2026.

Hello I'm Josh Darling, I'm standing as an independent but am a member of Restore Britain.

I was born and raised in Sheffield and live in Wincobank with my wife and two young sons. I am like many of you. I love my family, I love my city and I love my country.

However, you have a duty to be honest with those you love and the truth is, our country and our city is in decline and has been for some time.

The Labour, Green and Liberal Democrats have been in charge of the council for the entirety of this time.

I witness daily how the council wastes your money, is permissive of crime in our communities and seems to have forgotten who they are supposed to be serving... Ordinary, hardworking, British people.

Currently, our ward has two Labour councillors. Do we really need a third to just agree with the status quo?

If elected, I will be a true voice for the people in Shiregreen and Brightside who feel like their concerns are not being heard.

We need somebody truly outside the establishment to hold them to task for their mismanagement which makes everyone's life harder than it should.

I’ve been part of the Sheffield community for over seven years and live in Southey ward in Wadsley Bridge. It’s where I’ve lived, worked, and built my life, so it means a lot to me.

I like to stay busy, keep things simple, and focus on doing things properly. I value honesty, consistency, and just being straightforward with people.

Ian has lived in Stannington all of his life and has seen many changes, not many of them for the better.
He knows the problems that are faced daily by underrepresented residents who feel let down and ignored by successive councils.
It is time that someone finally stood up and made a difference and on May 7th, you have the opportunity to help make that difference.
Sheffield needs reforming.
Sheffield needs Reform.

Maz Hamilton is the Green Party candidate for Stannington Ward in the local elections on May 7th 2026

Maz Hamilton is a small business owner and recovering journalist who lives in Stannington. Their involvement in local politics stems from experiences of homelessness in youth, and from experiencing the life-changing power of well-run and generous social care, education and mental health services.

Maz is now an active fundraiser for social justice causes, including through their publishing business which supports anti-racist charities in the UK, trans rights groups and global democratic causes. They also campaign for carbon negative publishing and for small business initiatives that keep money in local economies. They are a keen walker, reader and crocheter, and have recently dug their first pond.

I live locally with my wife and three children. I've been a local farrier (maker and fitter of horseshoes) since 1998, supporting local farmers and residents.

I am currently a parish councillor where I was originally elected as an independent, but I joined the Lib Dems in 2023. Locally I saw our green belt under threat, poor bus services, and a rise in crime in our area, but local residents were being ignored by our Labour councillors.

Even recently they've just voted alongside Green party councillors to concrete over our green belt against the wishes of residents!

That's why I joined the Lib Dems and why I'm standing in this by-election. We deserve a strong local councillor who alongside 27 Lib Dem colleagues in the Town Hall can challenge Labour, not a single Reform councillor with no power or influence.

I hope you'll lend me your vote on June 26th so we can get one step closer to new leadership on our council.

I moved to Sheffield with my family in 2004 and live in Oughtibridge. It’s a city we love and in recent years I’ve been involved in local and city-wide issues including school governance and music education. I work in IT as a software developer.

I’m supporting the Don Valley Railway Group now bidding for Government funding for reopening of closed railway lines. This viable project to reopen the line from Sheffield Victoria to Stocksbridge with a commuter service every 30 minutes will make a massive difference for travel to and from Sheffield city centre.

This is what I think and policies I believe in:

Starmer, Badenoch, Farage - WE CAN’T TRUST ANY OF THEM!
Labour aren’t Labour anymore.
Reform are more Tory than the Tories.
Fund Welfare not Warfare
Tax the Super-Rich, Don’t rob pensioners and the disabled
For Jobs, Homes & Services - Against Racist Division

Liberty Steel should be nationalised straight away to save jobs and green steel and socially useful production

All public transport should be brought into public ownership for an integrated, cheap or free, environmentally friendly public transport system.

Support the community protests against the Council's Local Plan proposals to allow private developers to build on Wharncliffe Side Green Belt land. Campaign for a mass council house building programme (on brownfield sites) supported by the provision of public services.

Fund Peoples Welfare not Arms spending and Warfare - Restore the Winter Fuel Allowance and Stop proposed cuts to disability benefits and PIP.

My name is Andy Davies. I’m the Walkley Green Party candidate for the 7th May local elections. I’m proud to be part of the vibrant and welcoming community of Walkley. I’ve been a Green Party member since 2019. Like many of you, I became fed up with empty promises and little action from Labour locally and nationally. Instead, I joined the Greens because they better reflect my values of fairness, equality, and honesty in politics.

*I have the skills and experience for the job*

I run my own environmental grounds maintenance service, transforming neglected spaces into places that support both wildlife and people. I bring this same practical, hands-on approach to my activism across Walkley. In the ward I have raised and pursued repairs to potholes on Ashberry Road and challenged fly-tipping across Upperthorpe. I have also campaigned for better public transport and already led a petition to create more green space in the ward on Trickett Road.

*I will always stand up for Walkley residents*

As a councillor, I would always put the residents of Walkley first. Over the past year, I’ve supported Walkley food bank collections, organised community petitions and fundraisers, joined litter picks, and regularly door-knocked to listen to residents’ concerns. Unlike Labour, I act on residents’ wishes, supporting the nationalisation of Yorkshire Water and fundraising for the people of Gaza.

*I can win and bring hope back to Sheffield*

Across the country, the Green Party is growing as more people respond to Zack Polanski’s vision of bringing hope back into politics. In Sheffield, that means delivering real local results, standing up for our communities, protecting public services, creating secure green jobs, and ensuring everyone has access to decent housing, clean air, and a strong local economy. Sheffield deserves a future shaped by the people who live in it.

I live in Upperthorpe and I have lived around Walkley and Upperthorpe for nearly 20 years. I’m proud to live here and proud to be your local councillor.

Together with my colleagues Councillor Laura McClean and Councillor John Wright, I have a track record of getting things done locally. I have secured £2.7m investment to upgrade the Zest Centre. I’m holding Yorkshire Water to account for their leaking pipes. I have secured new pedestrian crossings and investment for affordable housing. New council homes are being built in Walkley right now. And as Leader of the Council, I agreed the deal to keep the World Snooker Championships in Sheffield until at least 2045.

My priorities are:
- **ENDING bus privatisation** and bringing public transport back under public control. And Labour will bring in **FREE** bus and tram travel for everyone aged 18 and under.
- **BUILDING new homes** with 1,000 new council houses that are safe, secure and affordable. 
- **INVESTING in solar panels for schools** to cut school energy bills so that more money can be invested in children’s education. Walkley Primary is one of the first to benefit.
- **ALWAYS opposing racism and hate and celebrating our city’s diversity.**

Sheffield is moving forward. But this is at risk. Reform UK are standing across Sheffield, including here in Walkley ward. They would rip our communities apart and take us backwards. I’m proud to live in a welcoming, inclusive community. We have to defend this and as your councillor, I always will. Vote for me to stop Reform. We can't let them in.

This election isn't about Westminster. It's about our streets, our communities and our city. My track record shows that I get things done and work hard for you all year round. I will always be a local champion for Walkley, Upperthorpe and Netherthorpe.

I’m asking for your support on May 7th. A vote for me is a vote for unity and a practical plan to get things done.

**Vote to stop Reform. Vote for a local champion. Vote Tom Hunt**

I welcome the chance to finally get rid of this hated Tory government but have no confidence that a Starmer Labour government will be much different. 

Starmer has watered down every Jeremy Corbyn manifesto commitment and promised “iron-clad fiscal responsibility” which means a continuation of 14 years of Tory austerity.

But the Greens are not a radical alternative - here in Sheffield they have shown that they are part of the establishment by joining the “co-operative council” with Labour and the Lib-Dems that continues to carry out Tory cuts in local services.

TUSC is standing as a voice for working class and young people disillusioned by all the establishment politicians and wanting a radical socialist alternative to the austerity and warmongering policies of the pro-capitalist parties.

I am a young trade unionist and community campaigner. I have experience in unionising low wage, zero hour and part time workers and am currently  involved in the Hillsborough Save Our Parks campaign organising community resistance to the privatisation of Sheffield’s green spaces. I have demonstrated against the Israeli state terror on Gaza and call for stopping arms sales to Israel and an end to the occupation of Palestine.

If elected, I pledge to only accept the average workers’ wage, not the £91,000 MPs’ salary! I will be a Socialist MP on a Workers Wage.

I moved to Sheffield in 2018 and quickly found my home in Grenoside, where I live with my family. I hold a PhD in Chemistry from the University of Warwick, and during my time there I helped to establish the charity UniBrass, which now delivers the Inter-University Brass Band Championships and the National Universities Brass Band Summer School—the largest student-run participatory music event in the UK.
I have since served as a trustee for several Music charities and am Current Chair of A.I.M.S a national charity supporting MS patients access cutting edge treatments. My professional career has been rooted in the charity sector, leading in the arts and community development both across the UK and internationally, including in Uganda. I currently work as a charity consultant in the local area supporting small charities with fundraising and Governance. Throughout this work, my focus has always been on helping people live more meaningful, enriched lives, an approach that will be at the heart of my work as a councillor for West Ecclesfield.

"I live not far from the ward near Hillsborough football ground. I teach English as a foreign language at the University Of Sheffield. I cycle to work and I’m an active member of Carbon Neutral University, a network of students, staff and the general public working on environmental and climate change issues."

I am 47 years old – married with four children and one granddaughter – and have lived in Oughtibridge since
2003.
For the last twenty five years I have devoted an enormous amount of time working for our communities,
including:
• Elected as city councillor between 1999-2003.
• Representing Oughtibridge on Bradfield Parish Council between 2010-2015.
• Chair of Bradfield Parish Council 2014-2015.
• A Governor of Sheffield Children’s Hospital 2015-2018.

I am a local person who wants to fight for the interests of this area. My #1 priority will be doing everything in my power to stop the current plan to build on the greenbelt. I will also work to ensure that you keep as much of your hard earned money as is possible and that your taxes are well spent on the things that matter like combating crime and fixing potholes instead of being wasted on vanity projects. I will also work tirelessly to promote not only my ward but the great City of Sheffield which doesn't shout about itself enough!

My name is Danny Allsebrook and I want to tell you about myself and why I want to be your councillor in Woodhouse and Handsworth.
 
My wife is Woodhouse born and bred. We live in a council house in Shiregreen now, but my father-in-law still lives on Coisley Hill and my uncle on Handsworth Rd we have family on the badger estate and friends who live in handsworth. Improving facilities in the ward matters to me personally.
 
Being a council tenant, I understand how frustrating it can be to get things done, and that’s also a big part of why I decided to have a go as a councillor myself. I want good quality services for me, my family and for our communities. I know anti-social behaviour can be a blight and why people often don’t report it, because they are afraid of reprisals or because they’ve found reporting doesn’t change anything. I decided I can’t just complain about what’s wrong, I’d better get stuck in to help sort things out.
 
I am passionate about green space and public parks. I have ten years experience volunteering with the Friends of Wincobank Hill. I have saved historic woodland from development and organised special events, like our annual lantern parade, which people really enjoy.
 
I stand for transparency, commitment and community. It’s not just a slogan, it’s a promise.
 
Transparency – in my experience, people have lost a lot of trust in politicians and feel they are not being properly informed. I will always try to deliver what you want, but if this is not possible you will get an honest and transparent explanation.
 
Commitment – I will hold regular surgeries and be a visible presence in the community, so you get to know me and feel you can ask me for a hand if you need one.
 
Community – I know people sometimes feel we have lost a sense of community. I will use my experience to help our community groups, listening and supporting wherever I can, including with getting funding.
 
If you have seen my show at Woodhouse Library you will know I’m also an

From my very early years, my parents’ sense of public duty – as a police officer and health & social care worker – instilled in me values based on fairness and community care. My work, first in community arts, then in academia, and now as a software developer, means I am used to working within tight budgets, navigating complex development processes, understanding cutting-edge digital technology, delivering world-leading research, and developing award-winning products. I have done everything from running a 25-person business, working for huge multinational companies, to freelancing paycheque to paycheque: I understand what it means to be precarious as well as to have the responsibility of others’ livelihoods in my stewardship.

Both Labour and the Conservatives tell us they see the problems we face, but neither is truthful about the scale of them. Reform tells us they see the scale of our problems, but their solutions are based on targeting scapegoats while they sell off what little is left of the NHS and our country, all to line the pockets of their rich backers. Only the Greens are brave enough to name the scale of the problem, and honest about the kinds of solutions that will help everyone, not just a few. The Green Party puts local people and workers at its heart. Sets out real possibilities to increase wages, lower bills, stimulate the economy through green jobs, and build cities your children can feel safe in, connecting towns and villages with public transport and walkable infrastructure, which improves our lives, our health, and our wellbeing.

I have always believed in workers’ rights, the need for justice for all, to build a society that lifts us all up, and an NHS and state that pick us up when we stumble. I also believe that the most urgent threat to our ability to improve our lives is the climate emergency – something which is felt deeply in the cost of living: energy, fuel, the cost of our supermarket shop – not just in the larger-scale climate events l

LABOUR ISN’T LABOUR ANYMORE! VOTE FOR A REAL SOCIALIST

The Trade Unionist and Socialist Coalition (TUSC) believes Labour has abandoned working class people. We need a new party that represents our interests. 

If elected we will campaign and fight for:  Fully fund the NHS and public services. Stop and reverse privatisation	 Mass council house building programme. Rent control NOW  No to cuts and council tax rises. A Peoples Budget drawn up by local people  NOT politicians 	 Renationalise rail, buses, mail, water, energy and steel  Free education and training  Restore the Winter Fuel Allowance – the council should make good the loss of Winter Fuel Allowance by using the Household Support Fund for pensioners in Sheffield  Inflation proof pay rises for all. A £15/hour minimum wage now! 

FIGHT FOR SOCIALIST CHANGE – TAKE THE WEALTH OFF THE RICHEST 1%!
